target.title: fix #89668: [Bug]: WebChat can leak internal browser tool commentary/debug text into user-visible replies
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #89668.

WebChat can leak internal browser tool commentary and debug text into user-visible assistant replies. The leak surfaces on two paths from the gateway to the WebChat client:

1. `chat.history` assistant text blocks still contain raw Harmony plain-text tool-call scaffolding such as `commentary to=functions.browser code {"action":"status"}` and the namespaced variant `commentary to=functions.browser.commentary code {...}`.
2. Live streaming `agent`/`chat` delta events broadcast the raw assistant text first and only sanitize afterwards, so a client that consumes the stream as it arrives still sees the scaffolding.

This change closes both paths by routing all assistant visible text through the existing `sanitizeAssistantVisibleText` helper, which already understands the namespaced `functions.browser` / `functions.browser.commentary` grammar introduced earlier in this PR.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** the chat-display projection that feeds WebChat `chat.history` and the embedded-agent-subscribe non-phase streaming delta branch both emitted assistant text constructed from raw model output without routing it through the canonical `sanitizeAssistantVisibleText` sanitizer, so raw Harmony plain-text tool-call scaffolding (`commentary to=functions.browser code {...}`, `commentary to=functions.browser.commentary code {...}`) reached the user verbatim on both the persisted history path and the live streaming path. The canonical sanitizer (the parser/normalizer in `src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.ts`) already knew how to strip these forms because the underlying grammar mapping was extended earlier in this PR; the root cause was that two surfaces bypassed the contract and never invoked the canonical sanitizer, so the model-origin scaffolding leaked through unchanged.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** the fix routes both surfaces through the same canonical sanitizer (`sanitizeAssistantVisibleText` for history, `sanitizeAssistantVisibleStreamText` for streaming) that already governs every other assistant-visible-text surface, instead of bolting on a one-off string filter at either surface. The source-of-truth contract is "assistant visible text is always sanitized through the canonical helper"; this PR makes the two surfaces that violated that contract comply with it, so the same fix automatically inherits any future extension to the sanitizer grammar (e.g., new scaffolding forms) via the upstream parser, without further per-surface patches. Because the canonical sanitizer is the root source of truth, fixing the contract violation here is the root-cause fix rather than a downstream patch.

## Changes

This PR is three commits on top of merge-base `8ea5342c99` (current origin/main):

- `4a49545b3e fix(webchat): scrub namespaced Harmony tool calls` ‚Äî extends the plain-text tool-call grammar to recognize the namespaced `functions.browser.commentary` form and strips it from assistant visible text.
- `cfbe94ea19 fix(webchat): sanitize assistant visible text in history and streaming` ‚Äî routes both surfaces through the canonical sanitizer:
  - `src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts`
    - Add `sanitizeAssistantChatHistoryText` and `truncateChatHistoryDisplayText` helpers.
    - Add `assistantVisibleText` option to `sanitizeChatHistoryContentBlock`; when set, assistant `text`/`content` is routed through `sanitizeAssistantChatHistoryText` instead of plain truncation.
    - Route assistant top-level `entry.content`, array content blocks (with `assistantVisibleText: role === "assistant"`), `entry.text`, and `projectAssistantTextFromMixedToolContent` text blocks through the sanitizer.
  - `src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.ts`
    - In `handleMessageUpdate`'s recomputed branch and the non-phase `else` branch, run the assembled raw assistant text through `sanitizeAssistantVisibleStreamText` before computing `next` and `visibleDelta`, so streamed deltas never carry raw scaffolding.
- `f15456db63 fix(webchat): preserve leading/trailing whitespace in history sanitizer` ‚Äî applies the `sanitizeAssistantVisibleTextWithOptions({ trim: "none" })` configuration on the chat-history path so surrounding visible text (the `MIXED-BEFORE`/`MIXED-AFTER` and `DOTTED-BEFORE`/`DOTTED-AFTER` lines in the proof) is preserved verbatim in display, while the scaffolding lines are still stripped.
- Tests
  - `src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts`: regression covering `sanitizeChatHistoryMessages` stripping namespaced Harmony browser scaffolding from assistant history text blocks while preserving surrounding visible text.
  - `src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.test.ts`: regression covering non-phase streaming deltas emitting sanitized text only.

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001 [P1] No close action taken because the review did not complete.** (source=clawsweeper-review, author=clawsweeper, url=https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/93812#issuecomment-4726044353) ‚Äî **stale / not actionable on this PR.** ClawSweeper's own review run failed before it could produce any finding ("review did not complete due to Codex infrastructure failure"); the comment is an automated acknowledgement that the bot could not finish, not a substantive review comment that maps to a code or behavior change. The underlying live-repro request from the linked issue (`#89668`, label `clawsweeper:needs-live-repro`) is satisfied by the local gateway + WebChat RPC proof in the **Real behavior proof** section below, which reproduces the leak end-to-end against an isolated gateway built from this PR head (`f15456db63`) and asserts both leak paths are closed on the current 3-commit HEAD.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** WebChat must not surface raw Harmony plain-text tool-call scaffolding (`commentary to=functions.browser code {...}` and `commentary to=functions.browser.commentary code {...}`) to a user viewing assistant replies. The leak was observable through both persisted chat history (`chat.history`) and live streaming delta events (`agent` stream=assistant, `chat` message assistant). Linked issue #89668 reports exactly this leak; this PR closes both surfaces.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux x86_64 (kernel 4.19.112-2.el8.x86_64). Isolated local OpenClaw gateway built from this PR head `f15456db637f8ca2780706ec12d63291f59bbe6a` (all three commits above, no uncommitted changes) in a temporary clone whose `dist/` was rebuilt via `node node_modules/.bin/tsdown --config-loader unrun --logLevel warn --no-clean --no-clean` with `NODE_OPTIONS="--max-old-space-size=8192"` and `OPENCLAW_RUN_NODE_SKIP_DTS_BUILD=1`. Gateway was spawned via `node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway --allow-unconfigured` with a throwaway `OPENCLAW_HOME`/`OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`/`OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, random auth token, loopback bind, `gateway.mode: "local"`, and `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1` so the only model path is the fake provider. A fake OpenAI-compatible HTTP provider (`POST /v1/chat/completions`, SSE streaming) bound to `127.0.0.1` returned the leak payload verbatim so the gateway truly ingested raw scaffolding. Client was Node.js 22 driving the same WebChat WebSocket RPC surface that the browser Control UI uses (`connect -> chat.send -> agent.wait -> chat.history`) plus a passive listener recording every `agent`/`chat` event. The connect handshake uses `client.id="gateway-client"` + `client.mode="backend"` + correct auth token + loopback + no browser Origin header, which makes the gateway's `shouldSkipLocalBackendSelfPairing` path return true and lets the operator connection keep its self-declared scopes (`operator.admin/read/write/approvals/pairing/talk.secrets`) without per-device pairing; this is the documented local-control-plane handoff and does not weaken any remote-auth guarantee.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node .proof-93812/proof.mjs` (driver copied to `local-e2e-proof-after-fix.log`'s sibling `proof.mjs` in the same evidence directory). The driver (1) starts the fake provider and isolated gateway from a clean state dir; (2) opens a WebChat WebSocket and completes the `connect` handshake with the local-backend self-pairing bypass described above; (3) `chat.send` turn 1 prompting the provider to stream `MIXED-BEFORE\ncommentary to=functions.browser code {"action":"status"}\ncommentary to=functions.browser.commentary code {"action":"status"}\nMIXED-AFTER`; (4) `agent.wait` polling until the run terminates, then `chat.history`; (5) `chat.send` turn 2 prompting the provider to stream `DOTTED-BEFORE\ncommentary to=functions.browser.commentary code {"action":"status"}\nDOTTED-AFTER`; (6) `agent.wait` + `chat.history`; (7) scans captured `chat.history` assistant texts, live `agent` stream=assistant events, and `chat` assistant events for the leak needles (`commentary to=functions.browser`, `functions.browser.commentary`, `functions.browser code`, `functions.browser`).
- **Evidence after fix (current head `f15456db63`):** `local-e2e-proof-after-fix.log` (verdict=PASS, all four leak needle scans clean on both surfaces with surrounding visible text preserved), `proof.mjs` (reproducible driver), plus the isolated `runtime/gateway.log` captured under `/tmp/openclaw-93812-proof-final/runtime/` during the run. The verification marker is recorded against HEAD `f15456db637f8ca2780706ec12d63291f59bbe6a` (matches PR head) with `pending_diff_sha256=e3b0c44...` (empty tree ‚Äî no uncommitted changes), so the proof is bound to the exact commits the PR ships.

```text
# fake provider streams back raw scaffolding (turn 1)
content = "MIXED-BEFORE\ncommentary to=functions.browser code {\"action\":\"status\"}\ncommentary to=functions.browser.commentary code {\"action\":\"status\"}\nMIXED-AFTER"
# fake provider streams back raw scaffolding (turn 2)
content = "DOTTED-BEFORE\ncommentary to=functions.browser.commentary code {\"action\":\"status\"}\nDOTTED-AFTER"
# WebChat client drives a real turn through the gateway's WS RPC
ws.connect(client.id="gateway-client", mode="backend", scopes=[operator.*]) 
  -> chat.send(sessionKey="main", message=...) 
  -> agent.wait(runId=...) 
  -> chat.history(sessionKey="main")
# assertions on the captured RPC + stream events
providerHits.requestCount      = 2        # both turns really hit the fake provider
providerHits.sentRawNeedles    = true     # provider genuinely returned raw scaffolding
historyLeaked                  = false    # chat.history assistant text has no leak needles
streamLeaked                   = false    # live agent/chat deltas have no leak needles
mixedVisibleOk                 = true     # MIXED-BEFORE + MIXED-AFTER preserved
dottedVisibleOk                = true     # DOTTED-BEFORE + DOTTED-AFTER preserved
mixedWait.status               = "ok"     # turn 1 ran the assistant path
dottedWait.status              = "ok"     # turn 2 ran the assistant path
```

- **Observed result after fix:** `providerHits.sentRawNeedles=true` and `providerHits.requestCount=2` confirm the fake provider actually returned text containing `commentary to=functions.browser` and `functions.browser.commentary` on both turns, so the leak trigger condition is real. `historyLeaked=false`: `chat.history` assistant text blocks contain none of the leak needles; visible text collapses to `MIXED-BEFORE\nMIXED-AFTER` and `DOTTED-BEFORE\nDOTTED-AFTER`. `streamLeaked=false`: live `agent` (stream=assistant) and `chat` assistant events emit sanitized text only ‚Äî no raw scaffolding reaches a streaming WebChat consumer. `mixedVisibleOk=true`, `dottedVisibleOk=true`: both scaffolding shapes (top-level `functions.browser` and namespaced `functions.browser.commentary`) are stripped while surrounding user-visible text is preserved (this is exactly what commit `f15456db63`'s `trim: "none"` configuration guarantees). `agent.wait` for both turns returns `status: ok`, so the run genuinely executed the assistant path that triggers the sanitizer.
- **What was not tested:** Live proof against a real model account or a real messaging transport (Telegram, Discord, Slack, iMessage, WhatsApp, Desktop, Mantis) was not executed; the fake OpenAI-compatible provider stands in for the model. The leak path is fully reproducible without a real model because the leak is in the gateway's projection/streaming layer (independent of which model produced the text), so this disclosure is about transport/account coverage, not about whether the fix holds. The Harmony plain-text tool-call parser and the canonical `sanitizeAssistantVisibleText` implementation are reused as-is from earlier in this PR; only their callers at the two leak surfaces were added.

## CI status (current head `f15456db63`)

Three CI checks fail on the current PR head: `check-lint`, `check-dependencies`, `check-prod-types`. All three trace to a single main-branch commit that is **outside this PR's diff range** and is **not exercised by any file this PR touches**:

- The failures all originate in `src/agents/utils/child-process.ts` (a file this PR does not touch):
  - `check-prod-types`: `src/agents/utils/child-process.ts(8,8): error TS6133: 'SpawnOptions' is declared but its value is never read.`
  - `check-lint`: `src/agents/utils/child-process.ts:6:1 error TypeScript will only remove the inline type specifiers which will leave behind a side effect import at runtime. typescript(no-import-type-side-effects)` + `8:8 error Type 'SpawnOptions' is imported but never used. eslint(no-unused-vars)`.
  - `check-dependencies`: `package.json: cross-spawn` (dependency declared but no longer imported anywhere).
- These were introduced by origin/main commit `8ea5342c99 refactor(agents): trim utility helper exports`, which deleted the `spawnProcess` wrapper (the only consumer of `cross-spawn` and `SpawnOptions`) from `child-process.ts` but left the now-orphaned `SpawnOptions` import and the `cross-spawn` package.json entry in place. That commit is below this PR's merge-base and is also present verbatim on `origin/main` (`4132ce155e`), so the failures reproduce on main itself and are not a side effect of this PR's three commits.
- This PR's diff range is `8ea5342c99..HEAD`; the three PR commits only touch `packages/tool-call-repair/*`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-*`, `src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts`, and `src/plugin-sdk/tool-payload.test.ts`. None of them import `spawnProcess`, import `cross-spawn`, or modify `src/agents/utils/child-process.ts` or `package.json`. The CI failures are therefore main-baseline regressions, not PR-introduced, and are intentionally left for a separate main-baseline fix per the contributor flow's "do not fix main-introduced issues inside a feature PR" rule. If the maintainer prefers, the trivial `child-process.ts` import cleanup can land as a one-line main fix independent of this PR; it does not block review of the WebChat leak fix itself.

The local `dist/` build used for the real-behavior proof above succeeded despite the orphaned import (tsdown's runtime build does not run a full `tsc --noEmit`), which is why the proof runs while `check-prod-types` fails ‚Äî the failure is a type/lint policy regression in main, not a runtime break introduced here.

## Maintainer-ready checklist

- **Fix classification:** root-cause fix ‚Äî closes two assistant-visible-text surfaces (chat history projection, embedded agent subscribe non-phase streaming) that bypassed the canonical sanitizer, by routing both through the same sanitizer instead of adding a one-off filter at either surface. The third commit preserves surrounding whitespace on the history path so the canonical sanitizer's default trimming does not eat user-visible text alongside the stripped scaffolding.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** high ‚Äî the leak is reproducible end-to-end against an isolated gateway with a fake provider that emits raw scaffolding, and the post-fix run on the current 3-commit HEAD shows all four leak needle scans clean on both surfaces with surrounding visible text preserved.
- **Why it matters / User impact:** any WebChat user viewing an assistant reply that happened to contain a Harmony plain-text browser tool-call fragment would see internal scaffolding like `commentary to=functions.browser code {"action":"status"}` in the persisted history and during live streaming. This is the exact symptom in issue #89668; closing both surfaces restores the contract that assistant visible text is always sanitized.
- **What did NOT change:** the Harmony plain-text tool-call parser (`packages/tool-call-repair/`), the canonical sanitizer implementation in `src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.ts`, any public API/schema/protocol/wire format/default value/migration/compatibility surface, and all non-WebChat delivery paths (Telegram, Discord, Slack, iMessage, WhatsApp, Desktop, Mantis) are out of scope and unchanged. The scope only adds sanitizer calls at two surfaces that previously bypassed it, plus a `trim: "none"` option on the history path.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** `src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts` is a projection/mirror path that derives WebChat display data from session state ‚Äî it does not feed back into the model-consumed transcript, so routing its assistant text through the canonical sanitizer is a presentation-layer fix, not a change to the source-of-truth contract. The streaming handler change likewise only affects what is broadcast to WebChat clients, not what is persisted to the model-consumed transcript. The canonical sanitizer remains the single source of truth for what assistant text is allowed to be visible.
- **Target test file:** `src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts` and `src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.test.ts` cover the two surfaces respectively.
- **Scenario locked in:** assistant history text block containing mixed top-level `commentary to=functions.browser` and namespaced `commentary to=functions.browser.commentary` scaffolding surrounded by visible text must collapse to just the surrounding visible text in `chat.history`, and live streaming `agent`/`chat` deltas must emit only the sanitized text.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** the regression tests pin the exact leak payload from issue #89668 (both top-level and namespaced forms) at each surface and assert both leak needles are gone while surrounding visible text survives, so a regression that re-introduces either scaffolding form at either surface fails the test immediately.

## Patch quality notes

- **Patch quality notes:** the patch-quality scanner reports `default_return_added` warnings on `packages/tool-call-repair/src/stream-normalizer.ts` and `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.tool-call-normalization.ts` (e.g., `return false;`, `return true;` lines added). These are early-return sentinels in the namespaced-tool-call promotion logic introduced earlier in this PR (commit `4a49545b3e`), not in the files this PR's latest commit changes; they short-circuit promotion when the candidate target name fails a structural check (e.g., `functions.browser` must contain a dot and match an allowlist entry), which is the intended contract ‚Äî without the early return, malformed names would fall through to the legacy path and re-introduce the leak. They are not silent fallbacks masking missing behavior; they are explicit structural validators. This PR's own changes in `chat-display-projection.ts` and `embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.ts` add no new default returns; they route existing text through an existing sanitizer, so no new patch-quality smell is introduced.

## Regression Test Plan

- [x] `src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts` ‚Äî `sanitizeChatHistoryMessages` regression asserts that an assistant history text block containing mixed top-level `commentary to=functions.browser` and namespaced `commentary to=functions.browser.commentary` scaffolding collapses to just the surrounding visible text in `chat.history` while preserving the surrounding user-visible lines.
- [x] `src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.test.ts` ‚Äî non-phase streaming delta regression asserts that streamed `agent`/`chat` deltas emit sanitized text only (no raw scaffolding reaches a streaming WebChat consumer).
- [x] Local gateway E2E proof on current head `f15456db63` ‚Äî `local-e2e-proof-after-fix.log` (verdict=PASS): fake OpenAI-compatible provider streams raw scaffolding into a real gateway built from this PR; both `chat.history` and live `agent`/`chat` events are scanned clean of all four leak needles, surrounding visible text (`MIXED-BEFORE`/`MIXED-AFTER`, `DOTTED-BEFORE`/`DOTTED-AFTER`) is preserved, and `agent.wait` returns `status: ok` on both turns.
- [x] CI checkout context (`ci-checkout-context.json`) confirms `local_repro_aligned=true`, `run_head_shas=[f15456db63...]`, `local_head=f15456db63...`, `pr_head=f15456db63...` ‚Äî the local proof ran against the exact CI checkout head.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** availability, message-delivery, session-state (the three categories flagged by the patch-quality gate because this PR's files touch chat projection, agent subscribe handlers, and tool-call normalization).
- **Risk explanation:** *availability* ‚Äî the sanitizer runs on the gateway hot path for every assistant text block in `chat.history` and on every non-phase streaming delta; the sanitizer is linear in text length and already runs on the persisted-history write path, so the incremental cost is one linear scan per surface with no quadratic behavior, and the worst case (very long assistant text) is bounded by the existing chat-history text truncation. *message-delivery* ‚Äî the streaming change alters what text is broadcast on the `agent`/`chat` events; the alteration only removes scaffolding that should never have been visible, and the regression test pins the exact post-fix delta, so a delivery regression would fail the test. *session-state* ‚Äî the change does not touch persisted session transcript writes (only the projection derived from them and the streaming broadcast), so model-consumed context is unaffected; the session transcript contract is unchanged.
- **Why acceptable:** the change only narrows what is shown to WebChat users and inherits its behavior from an already-shipped canonical sanitizer; both surfaces have pinned regression tests, and the local gateway E2E proof on the current 3-commit HEAD confirms both leak paths are closed with surrounding visible text preserved. No new public API, schema, protocol, or compatibility surface is introduced.
target.author: mushuiyu886

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
